title: 👑 Elite challenge ⭐⭐⭐⭐: the language of relationships

questions.prompt: Ines wants to protect the environment. Which piece of advice is expressed correctly?

options.text: You should to recycle your bottles.

questions.explanation: "Should" is followed by the base verb only: You should recycle ✓. The common trap is "should to recycle", adding a wrong "to". "Recycling" uses -ing and "recycles" adds -s — modals never change the verb form.

questions.prompt: Distinguish a rule from advice. In a museum there is a sign: "Do not touch the paintings." Which sentence matches the sign?
questions.explanation: A sign that forbids something is a strict rule, not just advice, so we use "mustn't": You mustn't touch the paintings ✓. The common trap is "shouldn't", which only means it is a bad idea, not that it is forbidden. "Should"/"must" would tell you to touch them.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right adverb: "The two brothers were angry, so they argued ___." (angry)
questions.explanation: The adjective ends in -y, so the y becomes i before -ly: angry → angrily ✓. The common trap is "angryly" (keeping the y). "Angry" is the adjective, and "angerly" mixes in the noun "anger".

questions.prompt: Which sentence about a pet is completely correct?

options.text: You must take care of your dog gently.
You must to take care of your dog gently.
You must take care of your dog gentle.
You must taking care of your dog gently.

questions.explanation: Two rules meet here: after "must" the base verb "take" (no "to", no -ing), and the adverb of manner "gently" (gentle → gently) to say HOW. "You must take care of your dog gently" ✓. Option b adds a wrong "to", c uses the adjective "gentle", and d uses "taking".

questions.prompt: Read: "(1) In my opinion, we must protect nature. (2) I feel proudly when I plant a tree. (3) You shouldn't waste water. (4) I disagree with your idea." Which sentence contains a mistake?
questions.explanation: Sentence 2 has the mistake: this question asks you to spot the faulty one. After "feel" we use an adjective, not an adverb, so it must be "I feel proud when I plant a tree" ✓, not "proudly". Sentences 1, 3 and 4 correctly use "In my opinion", "shouldn't waste" and "I disagree".

questions.prompt: Only ONE of these sentences is completely correct. Which one?

options.text: You should respect your parents and listen to them carefully.
You must to help your friends when they are sad.
I am agree that we shouldn't drop litter.

questions.explanation: Sentence a is correct: "should + respect" (base verb) and the adverb "carefully" to say how you listen ✓. "Must to help" (b) adds a wrong "to" after the modal, "I am agree" (c) should be "I agree", and "speaks very good" (d) needs the adverb "well".
